title: LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications
hook: LaMDA set a new bar for conversational AI that actually feels open and safe.
promise: You will grasp how LaMDA makes dialogue models more engaging, factual, and safe by design.
prerequisite_note: Bring a sense for how language models generate text, and curiosity about what makes a good conversation.

glossary.plain: A system trained to have conversations with humans using natural language.
How engaging, sensible, and specific a model's replies are in conversation.
The ability of a model to avoid harmful or inappropriate outputs.
How well a model's responses align with real-world facts.
Adjusting a pre-trained model on a specific task or set of criteria.
Labeling data (such as conversations) to guide model training or evaluation.

cards.title: Conversations That Feel Real
body: Discover how LaMDA made dialogue models more engaging, factual, and safe by design.

cards.title: Why do most chatbots feel so fake?
body: Have you ever chatted with a bot and felt it missed the point or sounded off? Most language models struggle to hold natural, interesting conversations.
LaMDA was designed to change that. Instead of just stringing words together, it aims to make dialogue that is sensible, engaging, and grounded in fact.

cards.title: How LaMDA shapes better dialogue
body: LaMDA starts with a large language model, then fine-tunes it using explicit targets: quality, safety, and factuality.
By collecting massive annotated conversations, LaMDA learns to generate responses that are not only fluent but also safe and accurate.
cards.caption: LaMDA's training process makes each conversation safer, more engaging, and more grounded.

cards.title: How does conversation change?
body: Imagine chatting with a model before and after LaMDA's improvements.
cards.offNote: Replies often generic, sometimes unsafe or factually wrong.
cards.onNote: Replies are more engaging, safer, and factually grounded.

cards.title: Making quality, safety, and factuality explicit
body: LaMDA's key trick is to make quality, safety, and factuality explicit training objectives, not just hoped-for outcomes.
By gathering human feedback and annotations on these dimensions, the model learns to optimize for conversations that feel both natural and responsible.

cards.title: Balancing engagement and safety
body: As you increase safety, how does the feel of the conversation change?
cards.low: The model may generate risky or inappropriate content, but conversation feels more open.
cards.mid: Conversation remains engaging, but risky replies are filtered out.
cards.high: All replies are safe, but some creativity or spontaneity may be lost.

cards.title: What does LaMDA need?
body: Pick the ingredients that make LaMDA's dialogue so effective.

choices.label: Pre-trained language model
choices.note: Foundation for language understanding and generation.

choices.label: Human-annotated conversations
choices.note: Provide examples of good and bad dialogue.

choices.label: Quality scoring
choices.note: Measures how engaging and relevant responses are.

choices.label: Safety scoring
choices.note: Filters out harmful or toxic replies.

choices.label: Factuality scoring
choices.note: Checks if responses are accurate and grounded.

choices.label: Fine-tuning process
choices.note: Adjusts the model to optimize for these conversational targets.

cards.title: LaMDA's impact on dialogue
body: LaMDA outperformed earlier models on quality, safety, and factuality in both human and automatic evaluations.
Its conversations were rated more engaging, less likely to go off-topic, and much less likely to produce unsafe responses.
cards.caption: LaMDA raised the bar for what users can expect from conversational AI.

cards.title: Why LaMDA is a canonical paper
body: LaMDA showed that dialogue models can be trained to be not just fluent, but also safe and informative.
Its approach influenced how future conversational AIs are built, making quality, safety, and factuality standard goals.

recap.label: Start
recap.note: Begin with a large language model.

recap.label: Score
recap.note: Optimize for quality, safety, and factuality.

cards.title: LaMDA: Language Models for Dialog Applications
body: For the full technical details, see the original paper on arXiv.
